message:
chore(beads): file + close sys/stat.h conformance bead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1108,3 +1108,5 @@
 {"id":"bd-1erld","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc string.h pure functions diff matrix","description":"Differential conformance harness shipped in commit 631b5100 (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_string.rs).

Coverage:
- 9 pure functions: strchr, strrchr, strstr, strpbrk, strspn, strcspn, strnlen, memchr, memcmp
- 5-13 hand-curated edge-case inputs per function (empty, single-char, no-match, match-at-start/end, embedded NUL, high bytes, overlapping needles, bound-cuts-match, bound-includes-match, usize::MAX bound)
- Comparisons: offset-from-base for pointer returns, exact length for size_t returns, sign-of-result for memcmp
- 0 divergences observed

Result: string.h pure-function subset is conformant against glibc.

Out of scope (mutating string functions deferred to subsequent harness):
- strcpy/strncpy/strcat/strncat/strerror/strerror_r/strdup/strndup/strtok_r

Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_conformance_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test conformance_diff_string","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped in commit 631b5100; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
 {"id":"bd-8kc11","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc stdlib.h numeric diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped in commit 0bd439d2. 8 functions (atoi/atol/atoll/strtol/strtoll/strtoul/strtoull/strtod), 16-26 edge cases per fn, ~150 differential calls. Caught + fixed strtod ERANGE-not-set defect on overflow/underflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped in commit 0bd439d2; 1 divergence found and fixed (strtod ERANGE).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","stdlib"]}
 {"id":"bd-78nb4","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] strtod missed errno=ERANGE on overflow/underflow","description":"FOUND + FIXED via differential harness vs glibc (commit 0bd439d2).

strtod("1e400", &end) returned +Infinity (correct) but failed to set errno=ERANGE (POSIX violation). Same for strtod("1e-400", &end) which returned 0.0 without ERANGE. Glibc sets ERANGE in both cases.

Reachability: any program that uses strtod to parse numeric input and relies on errno to distinguish overflow-Inf from literal "INF" (or underflow-0 from literal "0") would silently misclassify. Common in JSON/config parsers and scientific code.

Fix in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs strtod(): after parsing, set errno=ERANGE if val.is_infinite() AND consumed prefix doesn't contain "inf" literal, OR val==0.0 AND consumed prefix contains any non-zero digit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 0bd439d2; differential test diff_strtod_cases now passes including 1e400/1e-400 ERANGE cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","high","stdlib","errno","conformance"]}

Zero divergences. Compares all 12 meaningful stat fields exhaustively + return + errno.
